message.content: The license check works locally in dev but throws in the deployed build: 'node:crypto is not supported in the Edge Runtime'. It's in proxy.ts.

content.text: Let me confirm where proxy.ts runs in the build output.

There it is. In Next 16 proxy.ts is the renamed middleware and runs on the Edge runtime, which has no node:crypto. So the Ed25519 license verification can't run there. The fix is to move license and entitlement checks into Node route handlers (app/api/*/route.ts run on the Node runtime) and keep proxy.ts to cheap cookie/redirect work only.

message.content: moved verifyLicense into the /api/entitlements route handler and proxy just checks a session cookie now. deployed build is green.

content.text: Good — that's the durable rule: node:crypto (and other Node built-ins) can't run in proxy.ts because it's Edge; gate entitlements in Node route handlers instead.

message.content: unrelated: when I first cloned this, npm install failed. took me a while.

content.text: That is expected — this repo is pnpm-managed (there's a pnpm-lock.yaml and no package-lock). npm's installer chokes on the node_modules/.pnpm symlink layout. Always use `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; that's worth writing down so the next person doesn't lose the same time.
